title: GRAPE Act
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on General Farm Commodities, Risk Management, and Credit.
plain_summary: The GRAPE Act tells a government agency called the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation to create special insurance for grape farmers. This insurance would help protect farmers who grow table grapes, wine grapes, and juice grapes when their crops get damaged by freezing weather. The agency has to make this insurance available to farmers within eighteen months after the law passes. They also have to write a report to Congress within two years to explain how their work is going.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on General Farm Commodities, Risk Management, and Credit.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Grape Research And Protection Expansion Act or the GRAPE Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Policy to Insure Table, Wine, and Juice Grapes Against Losses Due to a Freeze Event
sections.text: This section changes the Federal Crop Insurance Act by adding new requirements for grape insurance. Within one year of this law passing, the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation (the government agency that runs crop insurance) must do research and development work on creating insurance policies for table grapes, wine grapes, and juice grapes when they are damaged by freezing weather. The Corporation can do this research itself or hire qualified companies to do the work. If the research meets certain legal requirements, the Corporation must make this grape freeze insurance available to farmers within eighteen months of this law passing. This must happen even if other parts of the crop insurance law would normally prevent it. Within two years of this law passing, the Corporation must write a report for Congress. The report must explain what they learned from their research and describe what insurance policies they created. This report goes to the House and Senate committees that handle agriculture and government spending.
